validation_output.summary: Passed with no diff-check output; plan.md has no whitespace/conflict-marker issues.

guardrail_evidence.evidence: Locked planning decisions state recommended skills never auto-execute and never block phase transitions by default (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:16). The spec repeats that recommended bindings do not auto-execute (FR-003, FR-014, FR-021 in .specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/spec.md). Live workflow prompt policy keeps skill declarations as prompt-level guidance only and says they do not enable SkillTool globally (src/server/services/workflowToolPolicy.ts:306-333).

guardrail_evidence.guardrail: Recommended skills must not become default phase-completion gates.
guardrail_evidence.evidence: The plan says recommended skills never block import, phase start, or phase completion by default (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:16). The spec keeps hard gating out of scope and requires recommended skills not block completion by default (FR-003 and acceptance proof in .specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/spec.md). Current workflow policy exposes only submit_phase_completion as the workflow-scoped tool and keeps completion semantics separate from skill guidance (src/server/services/workflowToolPolicy.ts:299-333).

guardrail_evidence.guardrail: Workflow phases must bind to skills, not plugins as primary objects.
guardrail_evidence.evidence: Locked planning decisions and MP-004 require skill-primary binding with plugin identity only as provenance/dependency metadata (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:15, .specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/spec.md FR-002 and FR-010). The data model defines pluginName as optional provenance on WorkflowPhaseSkillReference, not the primary binding (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/data-model.md:5-17).

guardrail_evidence.guardrail: Exports must not bundle skill package contents by default.
guardrail_evidence.evidence: The plan explicitly requires dependency manifests without bundling skill package contents by default (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:18). The export contract says to include every phase skill reference but not skill package contents (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/contracts/workflow-phase-skills-api.md#Export Contract). The quickstart export scenario also expects no bundled skill package contents (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/quickstart.md:18-25).

guardrail_evidence.guardrail: Skill-derived permission/model/effort/tool/fork/shell/hook effects must stay behind SkillTool.
guardrail_evidence.evidence: The implementation constitution says SkillTool remains the only boundary that validates and invokes skills and applies skill-derived tool/model/effort/fork behavior (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:101-104). The task guardrail index and CA-004 map this explicitly to T001/T015/T016/T022 (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/tasks.md:45, 58). Live SkillTool code owns validateInput, checkPermissions, forked execution, allowedTools propagation, model override, and effort override (src/tools/SkillTool/SkillTool.ts:354-578, 623-832), while workflow policy only provides prompt guidance and tool gating and explicitly says skill declarations do not enable SkillTool globally (src/server/services/workflowToolPolicy.ts:179-193, 306-333).

guardrail_evidence.guardrail: Existing template unknown-field preservation, legacy skills[].reason, old export/import compatibility, and protected user-owned state must be preserved.
guardrail_evidence.evidence: The task packet anti-goals and does_not_remove list preserve unknown fields, old exports/imports, legacy skills compatibility, and SkillTool safeguards (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/task-packets/T001.json). The spec requires existing skills entries, including legacy reason fields and unknown fields, to remain readable and writable without data loss and old exports to remain accepted (FR-008, FR-024 in .specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/spec.md). The data model and contract both require unknown-field preservation and keep reason as legacy advisory data only (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/data-model.md:5-18, .specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/contracts/workflow-phase-skills-api.md:3-19, 47-54). The plan also preserves existing user-owned state rules and forbids weakening those boundaries (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:93, 107-116, 367-374).

guardrail_evidence.guardrail: Future implementation must follow TDD and same-area tests.
guardrail_evidence.evidence: The task list marks G-TEST-FIRST for the relevant implementation tasks and says to add failing tests before implementation (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/tasks.md:66, 83-84). The plan's Recovery And Validation Contract requires old-fixture coverage, resolver tests, negative runtime tests, desktop tests, focused checks, and final gates (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/plan.md:367-374). The project contract in tasks.md and spec.md also requires same-area tests and bun run verify for this feature surface (.specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/tasks.md:11-14, .specify/features/007-workflow-phase-skills/spec.md:331).
